meta.title: TechPulse
meta.tagline: Tech & AI news, hot takes, and who's saying what — from the builders you follow
meta.note: Built from your X Following timeline, filtered to tech/AI builders by profile. Click any story to see every take and what different people are saying.

stories.title: Human Judgment Is the New Developer Moat
stories.summary: A chorus of builders and educators agree: now that implementation is nearly free, deciding *what* to build is the scarce skill. Taste, architecture sense, and product empathy are the real differentiators.

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text: Human judgement in engineering is ironically even more crucial now. Deciding what to build. Deciding on the right architectures. Deciding whether you regenerate from scratch $$$ or reuse existing legos. Managing tech debt. You can do anything now, but you can't do everything.

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
the models are very capable. i'm not saying they're very smart. but the bottleneck with them is likely your own imagination

proof is so many companies demo their ai product with "it can schedule stuff for you"

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
The 3 main 'prep' activities in software development are:

- Discuss: figure out what to build
- Research: summarize world knowledge to aid discussion
- Prototype: build something to aid discussion

First question: did I miss any?
Second question: how was it not obvious that AI

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text: Yep. This is why I've pivoted to teaching product engineering: taste, systems design, judgement, user empathy, etc.

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text:
the biggest engineering bottleneck isn’t writing code anymore

it’s deciding what to build

you can ship anything now

you still can’t ship everything

stories.title: OpenCode v2: One Backend, All Interfaces Synced
stories.summary: thdxr shipped OpenCode v2 where TUI, desktop, and web all share the same backend, meaning resource usage is minimized and everything stays in sync. GitHub Copilot jumped on the same wave by adding AGENTS.md support to code review.

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
in OpenCode v2 all instances of the tui and desktop and web share the same backend

so everything is synced by default and resource usaged is minimized no matter how many windows you have open

takes.name: GitHub
takes.text:
Copilot code review now supports AGENTS.md files. 💡

Here's how to customize for more context-aware reviews. ▶️

takes.name: dax
takes.text: this is laser targeted at me

stories.title: Codex vs Claude vs Cursor vs Composer: Builder Verdicts
stories.summary: Builders are swapping notes on which AI coding tool actually wins. Codex's computer use stands out, Claude Code's /goal mode edges out Codex for some, and Uncle Bob is running real-world comparisons across all of them.

takes.name: Uncle Bob Martin
takes.text: composer 2.5 fast is pretty good, but I was running up quite a tab.  Grok build is not as good.  Codex is better so far.  Just my impressions.  Not much science.

takes.name: Micky
takes.text: codex's computer use is so elite, it doesn't even come close

takes.name: Layton Gott
takes.text:
Hot take: 

Claude Code /goal works better than the Codex /goal

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text:
composer 2.5 fast might be the only model that’s actually optimized for small tasks

everything else feels like overkill just to rename variables and fix a bug

takes.name: Thomas Trimoreau
takes.text: anyone still using Cursor?

stories.title: Claude Code Is Becoming the Builder's Default Tool
stories.summary: Builders are shipping real products end-to-end inside Claude Code — from branded carousel generators to fully built apps. The tool is moving from assistant to primary dev environment.

takes.name: Mike Futia
takes.text:
I just built a branded IG carousel generator in Claude Code 🤯

One brand URL + one product name = 6 finished carousel slides.

The kind an agency charges $2K to produce.

All inside Claude Code.

Perfect for DTC brands and agencies who need to post carousels every week but can't

takes.name: Clifton Sellers
takes.text:
One of my close friends just launched an app for putting your phone down and being present

Completely built with Claude

Pretty freakin cool

takes.name: Auny 🧡
takes.text:
everything my Claude is plugged into right now (so it doesn't just talk, it does the thing):

→ my vault (reads + writes my notes every session)
→ my X (pulls trends + drafts posts)
→ my calendar (books + checks events)
→ my newsletter + scheduler (drafts go straight to the

stories.title: Mythos AI Spooked Congress in Two Days
stories.summary: Builders are reacting to reports that a closed-door Mythos AI demo on cybersecurity rattled Congress in under 48 hours. The offensive/defensive dual-use angle is raising serious alarm.

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text:
Mythos / Sol cybersecurity capabilities are equally useful in an offensive as well a defensive capacity.

If adversaries get ahold of an equivalent offensive capability, it poses a serious threat to US companies that remain unaware of latent vulnerabilities.

In the meantime, I

takes.name: Order
takes.text:
Congress doesn’t get scared easily. 

One closed-door AI sesh with Mythos did it in two days.

stories.title: Everyone's Building Agent Frameworks — Nobody's Using the Agents
stories.summary: thdxr calls out the gap between endless agent framework chatter and actual deployed agents. The irony: the agents most people use daily are ignored while builders chase abstraction.

takes.name: dax
takes.text: everyone's happy to keep building agent frameworks while ignoring every single agent in the products they use

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text:
mcp still isn’t a real standard yet, and the ecosystem is basically still in discovery mode

everyone is experimenting with different ways to make agentic workflows actually work in production, and we’re still early enough that “best practices” change every few weeks

it feels

stories.title: Higgsfield MCP Brings Voice Cloning & Dubbing Into Claude
stories.summary: Higgsfield launched an MCP integration that lets Claude generate audio, clone voices, and dub content in 50+ languages — all without leaving the Claude interface.

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
Claude can now generate audio via Higgsfield MCP.

Voiceovers, voice cloning, and dubbing in 50+ languages, powered by Seed Audio 1.0 and ElevenLabs v3. 

All inside Claude.

stories.title: Cloud Subscriptions vs. Owning Your Hardware
stories.summary: thdxr loves paying a flat monthly rate and getting free GPU upgrades. Rasmic is building a home lab the old-fashioned way. icanvardar predicts your phone becomes a remote control for the cloud within a decade.

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
people really hate this idea and i understand why

but man i have loved this shift. i pay a fixed price per month, before i got anywhere close to "break even" they upgraded the GPUs to latest generation

also every game being pre-downloaded is also very nice

takes.name: Micky
takes.text:
home lab prep plan

> Mini PC
> Nas + Harddrives 
> GPUs

takes.name: Micky
takes.text: @thdxr nah unc, i'll own my hardware the old fashion way

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text:
prediction: 

in the next 10 years your phone will basically become a remote control for the cloud

all the serious processing will happen on shared servers that stay busy instead of sitting in your pocket doing nothing most of the day

you get better performance without the heat

stories.title: Why Pay for Vibecoded SaaS When Agents Do It in 30 Seconds?
stories.summary: Builders are questioning the value of generic SaaS when personal AI agents can replicate it on demand. The counter-argument: branding and distribution may be what separates winners from noise.

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text: what’s the point of paying for a saas that was vibecoded in a week when i can just prompt my own agent to do the exact same thing in 30 seconds

takes.name: Thomas Trimoreau
takes.text:
If everyone can create products now

does branding become
more important than the product itself?

takes.name: Thomas Trimoreau
takes.text:
Building an app today is 99% telling people it exists and 1% coding 

What's your opinion??

stories.title: OpenAI's Chat App UX Has a Subtle But Annoying Bug
stories.summary: Steve Ruiz (@steveruizok) identified a timing issue in OpenAI's chat UI where a hover popover blocks pointer events, causing accidental link opens. Google's AI summaries already handle this correctly.

takes.name: Steve Ruiz
takes.text: If I were designing the @OpenAI chat app I would delay the opening animation here by ~120ms. A single pixel hover triggers the popover, which blocks pointer events. I've accidentally opened links because I've wanted to select text. Every one of these links is a ux landmine.

takes.name: Steve Ruiz
takes.text: @OpenAI Just noticed this UX is done right in Google's AI summaries.

hotTakes.name: Can Vardar
hotTakes.text:
the biggest engineering bottleneck isn’t writing code anymore

it’s deciding what to build

you can ship anything now

you still can’t ship everything

hotTakes.name: Can Vardar
hotTakes.text:
google keeps shipping models that make you forget they exist

at some point you have to ask if they’re actually in the race

hotTakes.name: Can Vardar
hotTakes.text: macos is getting so bloated that windows is starting to look reasonable again

hotTakes.name: Auny 🧡
hotTakes.text:
the loudest people warning you that AI is dangerous usually never built a single thing with it

the ones actually using it every day aren't scared

they're busy 👩🏻‍💻

hotTakes.name: Can Vardar
hotTakes.text: if your product needs a tutorial it’s not shippable

hotTakes.name: François Chollet
hotTakes.text: Only Art and Science raise humanity to the sublime. But the magic isn't in the output. It's in the process of creation and discovery, and the experience of feeling and understanding.

hotTakes.name: spidey
hotTakes.text: if everyone are shipping, who are using ???

hotTakes.name: dax
hotTakes.text: being too early to something is just the same thing as being wrong
